hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity in the ECL2/TM5 pocket, while a negatively charged residue at position 6 will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) electrostatic interactions without compromising SSTR2 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The hypothesis remains focused on achieving SSTR2 selectivity through disruption of off-target interactions. However, the current designs are over-optimized for binding affinity, which may hinder selectivity. A balance between affinity and specificity is needed.

proposed_changes.rationale: Current ddG values are too low, leading to overly stable designs that may not allow for the necessary mutational flexibility to improve selectivity. Raising the ddG threshold will encourage designs with moderate binding affinity, allowing for better tuning of selectivity.

To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing mutations at these positions will help break off-target interactions while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) + Cys3/Cys14).
